2605.13452.title: CUBic: Coordinated Unified Bimanual Perception and Control Framework
2605.13452.paper_summary: Recent advances in visuomotor policy learning have enabled robots to perform control directly from visual inputs. Yet, extending such end-to-end learning from single-arm to bimanual manipulation remains challenging due to the need for both independent perception and coordinated interaction between arms. Existing methods typically favor one side -- either decoupling the two arms to avoid interference or enforcing strong cross-arm coupling for coordination -- thus lacking a unified treatment. We propose CUBic, a Coordinated and Unified framework for Bimanual perception and control that reformulates bimanual coordination as a unified perceptual modeling problem. CUBic learns a shared tokenized representation bridging perception and control, where independence and coordination emerge intrinsically from structure rather than from hand-crafted coupling. Our approach integrates three components: unidirectional perception aggregation, bidirectional perception coordination through two codebooks with shared mapping, and a unified perception-to-control diffusion policy. Extensive experiments on the RoboTwin benchmark show that CUBic consistently surpasses standard baselines, achieving marked improvements in coordination accuracy and task success rates over state-of-the-art visuomotor baselines.
2605.13452.paper_summary_zh: 最近在視覺運動政策學習方面的進展使得機器人能夠直接從視覺輸入中進行控制。<lb/>然而，將這種端到端學習從單臂擴展到雙臂操作仍然具有挑戰性，因為需要獨立的感知和雙臂之間的協調互動。<lb/>現有的方法通常偏向一側——要麼解耦兩隻手臂以避免干擾，要麼強制實施強的跨臂耦合以進行協調——因此缺乏統一的處理。<lb/>我們提出了 CUBic，一個協調和統一的雙臂感知與控制框架，將雙臂協調重新表述為一個統一的感知建模問題。<lb/>CUBic 學習了一種共享的標記表示，橋接感知與控制，其中獨立性和協調性從結構中自然而然地出現，而不是來自手工製作的耦合。<lb/>我們的方法整合了三個組件：單向感知聚合、通過兩個共享映射的代碼本進行的雙向感知協調，以及統一的感知到控制擴散策略。<lb/>在 RoboTwin 基準上的廣泛實驗表明，CUBic 始終超越標準基準，實現了協調精度和任務成功率的顯著提升，超過了最先進的視覺運動基準。
2605.13452.author: Xingyu Wang et.al.
2605.13452.authors: Xingyu Wang, Pengxiang Ding, Jingkai Xu, Donglin Wang, Zhaoxin Fan
